title: Novavax Product Pipeline and Technology Platform Overview
text: All of Novavax's assets are built using protein-based nanoparticles and proprietary Matrix-M adjuvant technology platform. Matrix-M is an adjuvant that stimulates a broader and stronger immune response and is made of naturally occurring compounds sustainably harvested from the bark of the Quillaja saponaria Molina tree. Novavax has two vaccines currently on the market: COVID-19 Vaccine (Nuvaxovid) built on Novavax's proprietary recombinant nanoparticle and Matrix-M adjuvant technology platform, developed in response to the COVID-19 global pandemic and is the only protein-based, non-mRNA option. This vaccine is now commercialized by Sanofi, Takeda and the Serum Institute of India. Malaria Vaccine (R21/Matrix-M) developed in partnership between the Serum Institute of India and Oxford University, R21/Matrix-M is powered by Novavax's adjuvant technology for the prevention of malaria. This vaccine is WHO-prequalified and distributed by UNICEF in endemic regions. The company develops vaccines for influenza, respiratory syncytial virus (RSV), avian flu, shingles, Clostridioides difficile, and malaria. Starting with core expertise in infectious disease, Novavax is investigating other therapeutic areas where they believe their technology could augment and improve upon current therapies. Novavax leverages a discovery model that is driven by artificial intelligence and machine learning, enabling the company to optimize product candidates' efficacy, safety and tolerability.
